The global financial markets operated in a state of absolute, massive hyper-anxiety. This massive corporate gamble was heavily predicated on the aggressive belief that future global air travel would rely entirely on massive, high-volume hub-to-hub routing.

## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On October 24, 1945, the incredibly massive, universally defining, and absolutely historic Charter of the United Nations officially and aggressively came into full international force. Following the absolute, horrific devastation and massive global slaughter of World War II, this monumental diplomatic treaty fundamentally established the massive United Nations organization. It was heavily designed to aggressively prevent future global conflicts, heavily promote absolute international cooperation, and fiercely establish a massive, rules-based geopolitical architecture for the entire modern world.

On October 24, 1929, the incredibly massive, heavily catastrophic, and globally devastating stock market crash universally known as "Black Thursday" violently struck the massive New York Stock Exchange. The massive, unprecedented panic selling aggressively plummeted the absolute market value, instantly wiping out billions of dollars in massive global wealth. This incredibly massive, profound financial collapse fundamentally and aggressively initiated the absolutely devastating, massive decade-long Great Depression, heavily altering the absolute economic and political trajectory of the entire 20th-century world.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On October 24, 1914, Lakshmi Sahgal, the incredibly massive, deeply revered, and fiercely revolutionary officer of the Indian National Army (INA), was officially born in Madras. Affectionately and massively known as "Captain Lakshmi," she achieved absolute, historical immortality by heavily commanding the incredibly massive, all-female Rani of Jhansi Regiment under the absolute leadership of Subhas Chandra Bose. Her incredibly massive, uncompromising armed struggle against the British Empire heavily cemented her legacy as a massive, towering symbol of Indian female militant defiance.

On October 24, 1984, the incredibly massive, technologically advanced, and absolutely critical Kolkata Metro (Calcutta Metro) officially and aggressively commenced its absolute, very first commercial operations. This massive, monumental engineering triumph absolutely became the very first massive underground rapid transit system in the entire Indian republic. The incredibly massive, highly complex subterranean infrastructure heavily revolutionized the absolute urban mobility of the densely populated metropolis, fundamentally laying the architectural groundwork for the massive modernization of India's transit networks.
